title: Addition of the adaptor to RNA substrates for 5′ RACE
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2h7ca","text":"Simple protocol for mapping 5′-P RNA termini by RT-PCR after the addition of a 5′ adaptor using T4 RNA ligase 1.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"7qn41","text":"Prepare samples, including controls, according to the aim of the experiment. Use the chart below to decide which enzyme is appropriate for pre-treatments of the RNA. For example, if only interested in mapping 5′ RNA ends, use T4 polynucleotide kinase with ATP and without ATP to infer the original phosphorylation state of the end of interest.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b4be8","text":"Setup of the phosphorylation reaction can be found here: column clean-up (e.g., Monarch RNA Cleanup Kits from NEB is optimal when interested in small RNA molecules \u003c200 nt, which is the usual exclusion limit in other steps: Mix the following components (10 μL): %s %s 5′ and 3′ ends of the 5′ RACE oligo are hydroxylated.
Denature for 2 min at 70 °C, place on ice.
Incubate using the following program: 60 min at 25 °C, then 60 min at 16 °C, and then for 120 min at 4 °C.
Purify the RNA from the RNA ligase reaction (e.g., trizol extraction or column clean-up).

Proceed to RT-PCR. For the RT reaction, use 250-500 ng of the purified adaptor-ligated RNA and random hexamers (or a primer of choice). For the PCR, use an upstream (forward) primer binding to the 5′ adaptor and a downstream (reverse) primer binding to the RNA of interest.
creator: Matus Valach
